# unifi_traffic_matching_list (Resource)

Manage a UniFi traffic matching list.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manage a reusable traffic matching list for firewall and policy references.
data "unifi_site" "main" {
  name = "Default"
}

resource "unifi_traffic_matching_list" "web_ports" {
  site_id = data.unifi_site.main.id
  type    = "PORTS"
  name    = "web-ports"
  ports   = ["80", "443", "8443-8444"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String)
- `site_id` (String)
- `type` (String) Traffic matching list type. Supported values: `PORTS`, `IPV4_ADDRESSES`, `IPV6_ADDRESSES`.

### Optional

- `ipv4_addresses` (Set of String) IPv4 addresses, CIDR subnets, or ranges such as `192.168.1.10`, `192.168.1.0/24`, or `192.168.1.10-192.168.1.20`. Required when `type` is `IPV4_ADDRESSES`.
- `ipv6_addresses` (Set of String) IPv6 addresses or CIDR subnets such as `2001:db8::10` or `2001:db8::/64`. Required when `type` is `IPV6_ADDRESSES`.
- `ports` (Set of String) Port numbers or ranges such as `80`, `443`, or `10000-20000`. Required when `type` is `PORTS`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import a traffic matching list by site and list identifier.
terraform import unifi_traffic_matching_list.web_ports <site_id>/<traffic_matching_list_id>
```
